title: Terpsichore turrialbae
taxonomy: Terpsichore turrialbae is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody fern family. This species belongs to the genus Terpsichore, a group of tropical and subtropical ferns. Terpsichore turrialbae is also classified as a member of the order Polypodiales, which includes various types of polypody ferns. Further classification within the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta also confirms its status as a type of vascular plant. As a member of the plant kingdom, Terpsichore turrialbae exhibits characteristics typical of ferns, such as a fern-like frond and spore-producing structures.

intro: Terpsichore turrialbae, also known as the Turrialba tree fern, is a species of plant in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties.
